entries.description: Confirmed prerequisites at session start: Set 046 closed 2026-05-26 (state-file shows completedSessions [1,2], status complete, lifecycleState closed). Operator answers via AskUserQuestion: (1) NTE budget $10 for Set 047, (2) S1 follows Set 046 S1 pattern — self-authored proposal then two-pass devil's-advocate cross-provider consensus then scope-lock then per-session spec authoring (per feedback_devils_advocate_default_for_roadmap_decisions), (3) no operator-locked deliverables to absorb beyond the five audit topics already enumerated in spec.md (shape-of-v4 normalization, cancellation-marker design, migration sequencing, one-shot v3-to-v4 migrator subsuming Set 046 S6 expansion, blocked-on-prereqs lifecycle).

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from not-started to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; currentSession=1; totalSessions left null (Set 046 S2 forward-only writer behavior — appropriate for a stub-mode set where the audit deliverable IS the session-count lock). Observation: the writer's emitted state file does not include a sessions[] array — surfaced as a Set-047-relevant data point for the v4 audit (the missing-sessions[] case is exactly what the v4 schema would derive top-level state from, so the audit will need to specify the writer's behavior when sessions[] is absent vs. empty).

Delegated grounding research to an Explore subagent. Subagent located: (1) 6 writer call sites — register_session_start (session_state.py:408), _flip_state_to_closed (session_state.py:847), mark_session_complete (session_state.py:1064), cancel_session_set (session_lifecycle.py:161), synthesizeNotStartedState (sessionState.ts:267), ensureSessionStateFile (sessionState.ts:288), plus TS cancelSessionSet (cancelLifecycle.ts ~250); Set 035 dual-write convergence confirmed. (2) Reader call sites confirmed at memory-cited lines — fractionFor (CustomSessionSetsView.ts:142-147), readSessionSets (fileSystem.ts:243-524 with reads spanning lines 274-476), readCancellationState (cancelLifecycle.ts:147-158), gate_checks startedAt read (gate_checks.py:648). (3) Needs-migration detector confirmed at fileSystem.ts:373-381 — fires on schemaVersion absent/<3 OR (schemaVersion==3 AND sessions[] not array). (4) Field-touch enumeration for totalSessions, completedSessions, lifecycleState, verificationVerdict produced. (5) Events ledger emits 5 event kinds via session_events.py append_event — verificationVerdict snapshot is NOT redundant with ledger (snapshot is point-in-time, ledger is audit trail). (6) Test-file shape coupling: 54 Python tests + 128 TS tests + ~35 Playwright specs; real fixtures live in docs/session-sets/*/session-state.json across 47 sets. (7) NO sessionLog[] field found in the codebase — memory note may have been from a consumer repo not this one. No discrepancies with memory-cited line numbers.

Mid-session operator directive: 'The lightweight workflow should use the same exact process.' Clarified via AskUserQuestion: Lightweight orchestrators MUST follow same process as Full for (a) model/effort identification, (b) session-set identification, (c) session identification, (d) state-file updates, (e) Session Set Explorer UX. Differences limited to: no AI router, no auto-verification, provides copyable review prompts, suggests (not requires) UAT/E2E for interface-bearing work. Hand-edit recipe ELIMINATED. Saved as project memory project_lightweight_uses_same_process_as_full.md; audit proposal must absorb this as a load-bearing premise. Substantial scope expansion to Set 047 — may spawn sibling Set 048 (lightweight-tier writer parity). Original audit topic #4 (Lightweight migrator) is eliminated by construction; replaced by new topics: writer-architecture for AI-router-less Lightweight tier (separate dabbler-session-state PyPI? or dabbler-ai-router no-router-mode?), copyable-review-prompt deliverable shape, suggested-not-required UAT/E2E pathway, bootstrap-doc revision plan.

Authored docs/proposals/2026-05-26-state-file-schema-v4-and-lightweight-parity/proposal.md (~430 lines, 28953 chars): §1 purpose, §2 operator-locked premises P1-P4 (the Lightweight directive), §3 code-state grounding from the Explore subagent report, §4 Group A v4 schema dispositions (A1-A5: shape-of-v4, cancellation marker, blocked-on-prereqs, migration sequencing, migrator scope), §5 Group B Lightweight-parity dispositions (B1-B4: package architecture, copyable-prompt surface, suggested-not-required UAT/E2E, doc-revision plan), §6 critical scope decision (Shape 1 bundled vs Shape 2 split-into-047+048 vs Shape 3 inverted) with Shape 2 as drafter's recommendation, §7 proposed 6-session breakdown for Set 047, §8 six bias-cautions for the verifier, §9 four open questions for the verifier, §10 memory-hook citations (12 hooks). Self-authored per the in-session router restriction (feedback_ai_router_usage); cross-provider verification routed at the audit's own end-of-session boundary.

Ran Pass A of two-pass devil's-advocate consensus via run_consensus.py driver. Primary read (route, gemini-2.5-pro tier 2): ENDORSE WITH REVISIONS — endorsed all 5 Group A dispositions and all 4 Group B dispositions; endorsed Shape 2 (split into 047 + 048); flagged 4 missing topics: rollback procedure underspecified, performance impact unverified, Q2 CLI back-compat ambiguous, prerequisites field schema underspecified. Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — flagged Pass A primary's own blind spots: (1) missed cross-cutting v4↔package-split interface as a topic, (2) Session 6 over-scoped (bundles schema-doc + bootstrap + close-out + publish), (3) package-split endorsed too uncritically (suggested considering single-package + internal modularization instead). Pass A cost: $0.0545 ($0.023 route + $0.0315 verify).

Ran Pass B (devil's-advocate framing) of consensus. Primary read (gemini-2.5-pro): ENDORSE WITH SPECIFIC BIAS FLIPS — flipped Bias 1 (single dabbler-ai-router package + --no-router mode wins over Path 1 separate package), Bias 3 (Command Palette primary + right-click secondary, both surfaces), Bias 6 (must recognize non-canonical Lightweight shapes); stood by Bias 2 (derive), Bias 4 (reader-first phased migration), Bias 5 (Shape 2 split into 047+048). Open questions: Q1 token-only, Q2 re-export, Q3 backup file, Q4 'no Set 048 audit needed'. Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — pushed back on (a) the Bias 6 flip (Pass B primary's premise was wrong — non-canonical Lightweight shapes do NOT live in this repo per the grounding report, only in consumer repos out of Set 047's scope), (b) the Q4 conclusion (Set 048 DOES need its own audit pass; Pass B primary's 'no' was based on conflating Group B with a Set 048 audit). The verify pushbacks on Bias 6 and Q4 were both decisive — net STAND BY proposal on Bias 6 and net FLIP Q4 (Set 048 will run its own S1). Pass B cost: $0.054 ($0.0263 route + $0.0277 verify).

Synthesized the 4 consensus reads into verdict.md. Final bias dispositions: FLIP Bias 1 (single-package + --no-router), STAND BY Bias 2 (derive), FLIP Bias 3 (both surfaces — Command Palette AND right-click), STAND BY Bias 4 (reader-first), STAND BY Bias 5 (Shape 2 split), STAND BY Bias 6 (canonical-only migrator — Pass B verify pushback wins). Open-question resolutions: Q1 token-only, Q2 moot under Bias 1 flip + converted to firm CLI back-compat requirement, Q3 backup file, Q4 Set 048 needs own audit (Pass B verify pushback wins). 4 missing topics from Pass A primary incorporated as additional scope on Sessions 2-5; 3 missing-topic flags from Pass A verify either dissolved by Bias 1 flip or accepted (Session 6 over-scoped → adoption-bootstrap.md revision deferred to Set 048). Cumulative S1 routed cost: $0.10851 of $10 NTE (1.1%).

Rewrote docs/session-sets/047-state-file-schema-v4-audit/spec.md from STUB to AUDIT-LOCKED with Session Set Configuration (totalSessions=6, requiresUAT=true, requiresE2E=true, uatStyle=ad-hoc, effort=high), §1 what this set ships, §2 operator-locked premises P1-P4 (carry forward to Set 048), §3 scope-locked decisions, §4 6-session breakdown with all additions from the audit (Session 2 performance benchmark, Session 3 rollback procedure, Session 5 prerequisites-field schema, Session 6 deferred bootstrap-doc revision), §5 deferred-to-048 deliverables, §6 non-goals, §7 cross-references. Stubbed Set 048 (lightweight-tier-parity) at docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/ with spec.md inheriting premises P1-P4 + 5 locked-from-Set-047 design decisions + 6 major deliverables + 8 open audit topics; session-state.json was auto-created by the extension watcher in non-canonical shape (missing sessions[]/completedSessions[] arrays — same observation as project_session_state_auto_creation_observed) and patched to canonical v3 shape.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from between-sessions to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; currentSession=2; sessions[2].status=in-progress; cumulative S1 spend $0.10851 of $10 NTE (1.1%) carried forward. Per memory feedback_budget_question_scope, did not re-ask the budget at session start.

Inspected the Python and TS reader surfaces: ai_router/progress.py (canonical reader path; synthesize_v3_from_v2 and read_progress already centralize v2/v3 dispatch); ai_router/gate_checks.py (uses _read_progress_or_none wrapping read_progress); ai_router/reconciler.py (reads session-events.jsonl, NOT state file — no shim coupling needed); ai_router/session_lifecycle.py (private _read_session_state writes back state on cancel/restore — should NOT normalize since the write-back must preserve raw fields); tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts (TS mirror of progress.py); src/utils/fileSystem.ts:readSessionSets (the high-level reader that builds the Explorer SessionSet records; reads sd.orchestrator, sd.startedAt, sd.completedAt, sd.verificationVerdict, sd.forceClosed from the raw dict); src/utils/cancelLifecycle.ts:readCancellationState (only reads state.status, which is preserved at top level in v4 — no shim coupling needed); src/providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts:fractionFor (pure in-memory function over the SessionSet record — no state-file coupling). Confirmed the D13 lint test (test_no_legacy_field_reads.py) allowlists progress.py so the new shim's top-level field accesses inside the helper are compliant.

Implemented normalize_to_v4_shape(state, spec_md_path) in ai_router/progress.py. The shim accepts v1/v2/v3/v4 input, returns a NEW dict (pure function), and emits BOTH per-session metadata (each sessions[i] gets startedAt/completedAt/orchestrator/verificationVerdict defaulted to None) AND derived legacy top-level fields (currentSession/totalSessions/completedSessions/orchestrator/startedAt/completedAt/verificationVerdict/lifecycleState) so existing readers consuming top-level fields work transparently against v4 writes. Two derivation directions: (v3 in) promote top-level fields to in-progress / most-recently-completed session, then re-derive top-level from sessions[]; (v4 in) per-session metadata is authoritative, top-level derived FROM it. Routed read_progress() internally through the shim so v4 files read identically to v3. Added SCHEMA_VERSION_V4=4 constant and exported normalize_to_v4_shape via __all__. Routed 818 baseline Python tests through the new read path: all pass.

Mirrored normalize_to_v4_shape in TypeScript as normalizeToV4Shape in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts with byte-for-byte field semantics + same SCHEMA_VERSION_V4=4 constant. Routed readProgress internally through the shim. Updated readSessionSets in src/utils/fileSystem.ts: (a) JSON.parse output is now `rawSd` and is piped through normalizeToV4Shape into `sd`; (b) v2-compat events-ledger merge hoisted above the normalize call so it operates on rawSd (since normalize guarantees sessions[] on the output, the post-normalize merge would never fire); (c) needsMigration detector reads rawSd.schemaVersion (not the normalized sd.schemaVersion, which is unconditionally bumped to 4). Confirmed `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. All 553 baseline TS unit tests + the existing fileSystem/readSessionSets/progress tests pass.

Wrote ai_router/tests/test_normalize_v4_shape.py (23 baseline tests) and tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/normalizeV4Shape.test.ts (22 baseline tests). Coverage: v3 input → v4 (schemaVersion bump, sessionSetName preserved, per-session metadata defaulted, orchestrator/startedAt promoted to in-progress, completedAt/verdict promoted to last completed, between-sessions orchestrator to last completed, status canonicalization, totalSessions/completedSessions/currentSession derived from sessions[], preCancelStatus + forceClosed passthrough); v4 input → v4 (per-session orchestrator authoritative, top derived from in-progress, verdict from last completed, stale top-level ignored when per-session present); v2 input → v4 (synthesizes then enriches; pure-counts v2 normalizes cleanly); errors (None raises TypeError, non-list sessions raises SessionStateInvariantError, no mutation — pure function); readProgress routing (v3 file works, v4 file works, strict v4 with only schemaVersion+sessionSetName+sessions+status works).

Authored tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/readSessionSetsPerfBenchmark.test.ts: walks the real docs/session-sets/ tree (47 sets), calls readSessionSets(repoRoot) 20 times after a warm-up iteration, prints p50/p95/max/mean wall-clock timings, and asserts p95 < 5000ms as a relaxed regression guard. Baseline emitted to test output: mean=21.8ms p50=21.2ms p95=32.5ms max=32.5ms (Windows 11 + warm SSD + node 22 + ts-node). The test will trip on a ~150× regression — tight enough to catch a real reader-side perf bug, loose enough to survive CI runner variance. Tightening the bound is a future-set conversation once we have stable CI numbers across Windows / macOS / Linux.

Ran routed cross-provider verification via docs/session-sets/047-state-file-schema-v4-audit/run_s2_verification.py. Bundle: progress.py + progress.ts + fileSystem.ts + the three new test files + perf benchmark (123,639 chars total). task_type='session-verification' routed to gpt-5-4 tier 3. Verdict: ISSUES_FOUND with 3 must-fix items. (1) Per-session status aliases not canonicalized in the shim's build loop — a hand-edited "completed" session would not appear in derived completedSessions[]. (2) Top-level startedAt lost on v3 between-sessions / all-complete snapshots and v4 derivation scanned from start of sessions[] (returns session 1's startedAt) instead of last-completed. (3) needsMigration should flag raw v3 files (verifier's reading of my own ambiguous contract property 3 wording). Cost: 215.4s, $0.25798, 29337 input + 12309 output tokens. NOTE: first run lost the response text because run_s2_verification.py grabbed `result.text` instead of the actual attribute `result.content` (per RouteResult dataclass) — fixed the attribute names per memory feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling; transcript recovered on Round 2.

Applied fixes for Issues 1 and 2 in both Python (ai_router/progress.py) and TypeScript (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts). Fix 1: call canonicalize_status (Python) / canonicalizeStatus (TS) on each entry's status field inside the build loop BEFORE downstream derivation reads it. Fix 2a: in the v3-promotion block, when no in-progress session, promote top-level startedAt to the most-recently-completed session alongside the orchestrator block. Fix 2b: in the top-level derivation block, when derived_started/derivedStarted is null and completed sessions exist, scan completed_v4/completedV4 in REVERSE so the most-recently-completed session's startedAt wins (was: scanning sessions_v4 from start returned session 1's startedAt on multi-session sets). Added 9 Python regression tests (TestVerifierFix1*, TestVerifierFix2*, TestVerifierNiceToHaveIdempotence) and 9 TS regression tests (verifier fix 1 / fix 2 / idempotence suites) covering both fixes plus the verifier's nice-to-have idempotence guarantee normalize(normalize(x))==normalize(x). Issue 3 (v3 needsMigration flag expansion) deferred to Session 3 with a documented code-comment rationale at fileSystem.ts: the dabblerSessionSets.migrate command is hardwired to "Migrate to v3 schema" and its set.needsMigration check early-returns for v3 files — flipping the flag now would point the badge CTA at a nonexistent migrator action. Tests: 32 Python shim tests pass (23 baseline + 9 regression); 31 TS shim tests pass (22 baseline + 9 regression).

Re-ran routed verification with the corrected RouteResult attribute names and post-fix bundle (139,183 chars). Verdict: ISSUES_FOUND with 1 remaining item — Issue 3 (v3 needsMigration expansion) only. Verifier confirmed Issues 1 and 2 from Round 1 are resolved (they are NOT re-listed). Cost: 186.7s, $0.22553, 31000 input + 17286 output tokens. Decision: accept ISSUES_FOUND verdict with documented deferral rather than burning another $0.20+ on a Round 3 just to flip a single flag. Per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex, this is not a spiral — Round 2 confirmed prior fixes; the remaining issue is a contract-wording-ambiguity disagreement, not a code defect. Round-1 + Round-2 routed cost for S2 verification: $0.48351. Cumulative S1+S2 routed cost: $0.59202 of $10 NTE (5.9%).

Full regression checks: (a) Python pytest — 850 passed (818 baseline + 32 new shim tests), 1 skipped (pre-existing), 0 failed. (b) Python e2e (ai_router/tests/e2e/) — 11 passed. (c) Python e2e marker tests — 8 passed. (d) TS npm run test:unit — 563 passed (553 baseline + 10 shim baseline + 9 regression + 1 perf benchmark), 2 failed (pre-existing failures unchanged from baseline: configEditor-foundation ViewColumn stub issue + notificationsSection disabled-button assertion, both last-touched in Set 026 — unrelated to v4 work). Perf baseline emitted: readSessionSets(47 sets) × 20 iterations: mean=21.8ms p50=21.2ms p95=32.5ms max=32.5ms.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from between-sessions to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; currentSession=3; sessions[3].status=in-progress; cumulative S1+S2 spend $0.59202 of $10 NTE (5.9%) carried forward. Per memory feedback_budget_question_scope, did not re-ask the budget at session start — confirmed continuation under existing $10 NTE.

Surveyed the existing v2→v3 migrator code as the template for the v3→v4 module: ai_router/migrate_session_state.py (Python bulk CLI with regex/generic/ai/interactive strategies, MigrationResult dataclass, _atomic_write_json via tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace), tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/migrateSessionState.ts (TS mirror with same MigrationResult shape), tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/migrateSet.ts (right-click command for dabblerSessionSets.migrate). Confirmed normalize_to_v4_shape contract from S2: shim handles v1/v2/v3/v4 input, returns v4 read-view with per-session metadata + derived top-level fields. Decision: v3→v4 migrator is much simpler than v2→v3 because v3 already has sessions[] titles — no strategy choice needed. Migrator becomes a thin wrapper around normalize_to_v4_shape that strips the derived top-level fields before writing.

Authored ai_router/migrate_v3_to_v4.py with migrate_one_set (idempotent + dry-run + apply modes, writes session-state.v3.bak.json BEFORE replacing state file), build_v4_on_disk_shape (drops 8 derived top-level keys: lifecycleState, currentSession, totalSessions, completedSessions, startedAt, completedAt, orchestrator, verificationVerdict; preserves schemaVersion=4, sessionSetName, status, sessions; passthrough preCancelStatus + forceClosed when present in original; canonicalizes status alias on the way out), discover_session_sets, migrate_all, _atomic_write_json (tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace mirrored from v2→v3), _atomic_copy_json (re-parse + re-emit so .bak gets indent=2 formatting), main CLI (--scan, --in-place, --only, --verbose, --json). Eight action enum values: migrated, skipped-v4, skipped-not-v3, skipped-no-state, skipped-malformed, skipped-future-schema, would-violate, failed-backup. Validation: normalize_to_v4_shape output passes through get_progress() to enforce the 8 invariants — a v3 file with status=complete but a not-started session surfaces as would-violate rather than silently producing an invalid v4 file.

Mirrored migrate_v3_to_v4.py as tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/migrateSessionStateV4.ts (migrateOneSetV4, buildV4OnDiskShape, atomicWriteJson, atomicCopyJson). Same action enum string values + same backup filename + same write order so the rollback procedure works regardless of which migrator wrote the .bak. Per-language naming convention preserved: snake_case in Python (set_dir, backup_path), camelCase in TS (setDir, backupPath) — these internal API fields are explicitly OUT of the parity contract per the module docstring. Added the dabblerSessionSets.migrateToV4 right-click command at src/commands/migrateSetV4.ts: modal confirm before migration; branched failed-backup messaging (rollback-needed vs no-bak); progress notification during the migration; refresh on success.

Added migrationTargetSchemaVersion: 3 | 4 | null field to SessionSet type. Expanded the fileSystem.ts needsMigration detector to flag canonical v3 files with target=4 (the deferred-from-S2 fix); broken-v3 / v1/v2 / missing-schema with target=3; v4+ files don't flag; future-schema (> 4) doesn't flag (no downgrade). Updated ActionRegistry to split the predicate: needsMigrationToV3 (group 801, 'Migrate to v3 schema') and needsMigrationToV4 (group 802, 'Migrate to v4 schema') — mutually exclusive by construction. Registered the new command in extension.ts (safeRegister) + package.json (contributes.commands) + COMMAND_ALLOWLIST in CustomSessionSetsView.ts. Updated test fixtures across actionRegistry, flagDecisionForReview, forceClosedBadge, inProgressSetsService, sessionSetsProvider to include the new field with null default.

Authored docs/v3-to-v4-rollback-procedure.md covering: when to use (4 trigger conditions including partial-failure dispositions, post-migration read-side regressions, pre-shim toolchain versions, operator preference); what the migrator wrote (session-state.json v4 + session-state.v3.bak.json); 3 procedure options (single-set via shell, single-set via VS Code right-click + rename, all-sets-at-once via shell loop with bash + PowerShell variants); 5-point validation checklist after rollback; failure modes during rollback itself; re-running the migration after rollback (idempotent so just re-run). Cross-references to spec.md, migrator source files, and canonical schema doc.

Authored ai_router/tests/test_migrate_v3_to_v4.py (29 tests baseline) covering happy-path (in-progress / completed v3 → v4 with per-session promotion + dropped top-level keys, status alias canonicalization, passthrough preservation, round-trip equivalence through the shim), idempotence (v4 skipped + apply-then-skip), refusals (v2 → not-v3, missing schema → not-v3, broken v3 → malformed, future → future-schema, missing file → no-state, unparseable JSON, non-object top-level, invariant violation → would-violate), backup behavior (apply writes .bak with original content, dry-run touches nothing, backup-write-failure aborts), build_v4_on_disk_shape pure function, discover_session_sets + migrate_all + CLI smoke. Mirrored in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/migrateSessionStateV4.test.ts (20 tests baseline). Added 6 needsMigration + migrationTargetSchemaVersion tests in fileSystem.test.ts. Added 3 ActionRegistry mutex / count-bump tests in actionRegistry.test.ts.

Full regression: (a) Python pytest — 879 passed (850 baseline + 29 new migrator tests), 1 skipped, 0 failed. (b) TS npm run test:unit — 591 passed (563 baseline + ~28 new tests), 2 pre-existing failures unchanged. (c) Watcher-inventory test caught a line-number drift after the extension.ts safeRegister block insertion (147 → 148); updated WATCHER_ALLOWLIST. (d) Real-world dry-run: python -m ai_router.migrate_v3_to_v4 --scan docs/session-sets --json over this repo's 48 historical session-set directories — 47 would migrate cleanly; 1 (048-lightweight-tier-parity) returned would-violate due to its empty sessions[] stub state file, consistent with the recurring v3-bug-tax observation from S1. Migrator's get_progress validation step working as designed.

Ran routed cross-provider verification via docs/session-sets/047-state-file-schema-v4-audit/run_s3_verification.py. Bundle: migrate_v3_to_v4.py + migrateSessionStateV4.ts + migrateSetV4.ts + fileSystem.ts + ActionRegistry.ts + types.ts + v3-to-v4-rollback-procedure.md + 4 test files (152540 chars total). task_type='session-verification' routed to gpt-5-4 tier 3. Verdict: VERIFIED with 0 must-fix critical items, 4 IMPORTANT items, 2 NICE-TO-HAVE items. Cost: 209.1s, $0.2882. Cumulative S1+S2+S3 routed cost: $0.88022 of $10 NTE (8.8%). VERIFIED on Round 1 — a step up from S2's two ISSUES_FOUND rounds; reflects the more straightforward scope of the migrator phase (thin wrapper around the audited-in-S2 shim).

Applied in-flight fixes for all 4 verifier IMPORTANT items (per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope): (1) tightened docs/v3-to-v4-rollback-procedure.md to exclude would-violate and no-bak failed-backup from the rollback trigger conditions; branched migrateSetV4.ts's failed-backup notification on whether result.backupPath is set; (2) hoisted the needsMigration + migrationTargetSchemaVersion block in fileSystem.ts to run BEFORE normalizeToV4Shape so a normalize failure no longer eats the badge; (3) added explicit parity-contract docstring to migrateSessionStateV4.ts narrowing the Python↔TS lockstep to on-disk shape + backup filename + action enum values (excludes internal field names); (4) added 2 new Python tests (state-write-failure-after-backup distinguishing the rollback-needed subcase + done-alias canonicalization) and 2 new TS tests (backup-write-failure using directory-at-bak-path trick to force renameSync failure + done-alias). Split the Python CLI's [BACKUP-FAILED] line into [BACKUP-FAILED:rollback-needed] vs [BACKUP-FAILED:no-bak] subtypes. Deferred: cross-language golden parity fixture (substantial test-infra; better designed alongside Sessions 4-5 writer flip). Final test posture: 881 Python (+2 new), 593 TS (+2 new); same 2 pre-existing baseline failures unchanged.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, provider=anthropic, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high. State-file flipped from between-sessions to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; currentSession=4; sessions[4].status=in-progress; cumulative S1+S2+S3 spend $0.88022 of $10 NTE (8.8%) carried forward. Per memory feedback_budget_question_scope, did not re-ask the budget at session start.

Surveyed the four Python writer surfaces (register_session_start, _flip_state_to_closed, mark_session_complete, cancel_session_set) + supporting writers (_not_started_payload, _backfill_payload) + the v4 shape contract from migrate_v3_to_v4.py build_v4_on_disk_shape + the shim normalize_to_v4_shape derivation rules. Confirmed top-level dropped vs preserved key sets, same-holder reattach logic, and the plan-less Set 046 carve-out.

Bumped SCHEMA_VERSION = 4 in session_state.py (re-exported from progress.SCHEMA_VERSION_V4). Added _V4_PER_SESSION_METADATA_KEYS + _V4_TOP_LEVEL_DROPPED_KEYS constants. Authored three helpers: _read_prior_v4_metadata (normalize prior state via shim to extract per-session startedAt/completedAt/orchestrator/verificationVerdict), _apply_v4_per_session_metadata (default + preserve per-session fields across sessions[] rewrite), _strip_v4_dropped_top_level_keys (defensive drop).

Flipped register_session_start to emit v4 on-disk shape per spec section 3.1. Same-holder reattach reads the existing in-progress session per-session orchestrator (v4) or top-level orchestrator (v3) - both paths supported during the transition window. In-progress session record gets startedAt + orchestrator block (engine, provider, model, effort, chatSessionId, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt). Prior-completed sessions preserve their metadata via _apply_v4_per_session_metadata + prior_v4_metadata lookup. Top-level dropped keys are absent. Plan-less carve-out keeps top-level orchestrator + startedAt for the documented absent-sessions[] case.

Flipped _flip_state_to_closed to emit v4. Reads raw existing state; falls back to sessions[] scan for in-progress entry when v4 input drops top-level currentSession. Closing session gets per-session completedAt + verificationVerdict; per-session orchestrator preserved as historical record (the v3 clear top-level orchestrator on close semantic now implicit in status moving off in-progress). Forced last-session promotion sets completedAt on every newly-promoted complete session. Top-level forceClosed + preCancelStatus passthroughs preserved. compute_effective_completed_sessions extended with sessions[] fallback for v4 input; mark_session_complete extended with sessions[]-scan currentSession fallback.

Flipped cancel_session_set + restore_session_set (session_lifecycle.py) via new _to_v4_on_disk_shape helper that projects any v1/v2/v3/v4 input through the shim and trims to the v4 contract. Updated _not_started_payload to emit v4 shape (drop top-level lifecycleState/currentSession/etc; per-session metadata defaulted to null). Updated _backfill_payload change-log and activity-log branches to write per-session startedAt on session 1 of the activity-log branch (rather than top-level startedAt).

Routed read_session_state through normalize_to_v4_shape so all downstream consumers (gate_checks, reconciler, CLI, tests) see a derived v3-style top-level view transparently regardless of whether the on-disk file is v3 or v4. Added read_raw_session_state companion for the writer-side / migrator-side raw-bytes accessor. Confirmed register_session_start + _flip_state_to_closed + mark_session_complete now use read_raw_session_state where they need the literal shape and read_session_state where they want the derived view.

Extended progress.normalize_to_v4_shape with five backward-compat derivations: (a) lifecycleState derived from canonical top-level status when input drops the field; (b) derived_total_sessions returns None when input was plan-less AND synthesized sessions[] is empty (preserves Set 046 0/? signal under v4); (c) plan-less step-4 fallback uses top-level orchestrator + startedAt when sessions[] is empty AND status is in-progress; (d) top-level completedAt derives ONLY when set-level status is complete (preserves v3 SET-completion timestamp semantic vs the last-session-close timestamp); (e) shim no longer falls back to last_completed.orchestrator for derived top-level (preserves v3 H1/H3 check-in semantic released between sessions).

Authored new test_session_state_v4_writers.py (12 baseline tests covering schemaVersion 4, dropped top-level keys absent, per-session metadata population/preservation, plan-less carve-out, cancel/restore v4 shape, force-promotion). Batch-updated 11 existing test files for v4 emission: test_session_state_v2.py + v3.py (schemaVersion bumps + shim-routed derived-view assertions), test_chatsessionid_writer.py + checkout_writer + takeover_prompt (seed helpers write per-session orchestrator; v3-style return-dict convenience for assertion compatibility), test_start_session.py (plan-less raw vs shim split), test_close_session_snapshot_flip.py (orchestrator-clear assertions updated to assert top-level absent on disk + shim derives None), test_read_status.py (lazy-synth raw vs shim split), test_normalize_v4_shape.py (status-gated completedAt update), e2e/fixtures.py (read_state shim-routed + read_raw_state raw), e2e/test_register_session_start_regression.py (completedSessions read via shim).

Rewrote scripts/dump_session_state_schema.py build_example_state() to produce v4 example (per-session metadata; dropped top-level keys); updated scripts/test_dump_session_state_schema.py to assert the new top-level key set + per-session shape. Regenerated docs/session-state-schema-example.json.

Full Python regression: pytest 893 passed (881 prior baseline + 12 new v4 writer tests), 1 skipped (pre-existing), 0 failed. e2e marker (pytest -m e2e): 8 passed. All four writer surfaces emit v4 on-disk shape; all downstream consumers transparently read both v3 and v4 input via the shim-routed read_session_state.

Applied in-flight fixes for all 3 Critical verifier items + 1 Important (per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope): (1) register_session_start now derives effective_total from prior v4 sessions[] ledger BEFORE falling through to spec/headings, so re-attach against a v4-only file (no spec) lands correctly; (2) _not_started_payload + _backfill_payload now consult ### Session N headings as a fallback when spec.md has no Session Set Configuration totalSessions block - mirrors register_session_start headings fallback; (3) _to_v4_on_disk_shape now detects the plan-less carve-out (input had no sessions[] AND normalized sessions[] is empty) and preserves the absent-sessions[] form + top-level orchestrator/startedAt across cancel/restore; (4) read_raw_state in e2e/fixtures.py now actually calls read_raw_session_state instead of the shim-routed read_session_state. Added TestVerifierCriticalFixes class to test_session_state_v4_writers.py with 3 regression tests pinning each Critical fix shut. Final test posture: 896 Python tests passed (12 baseline v4-writer + 3 verifier-fix regression + 881 prior), 1 skipped, 0 failed.

Flipped the four TS writer surfaces to emit canonical v4 on-disk shape. sessionState.ts: SCHEMA_VERSION imported from progress.ts as SCHEMA_VERSION_V4 (4); LazySessionRecord extended with v4 per-session metadata fields (startedAt/completedAt/orchestrator/verificationVerdict, all defaulted to null in buildSessions); notStartedPayload trims to v4 shape (just schemaVersion/sessionSetName/status/sessions); backfillPayload's change-log branch promotes all sessions to status: complete (per-session completedAt stays null per Python parity), activity-log branch promotes sessions[0].status=in-progress and writes the earliest activity-log timestamp onto sessions[0].startedAt; readTotalSessionsFromSpec falls back on ### Session N headings when the config block has no numeric totalSessions (mirrors Python S4 verifier Critical 2). cancelLifecycle.ts: new toV4OnDiskShape helper projects v1/v2/v3/v4 input through normalizeToV4Shape and trims to the v4 contract; plan-less carve-out detection (input had no sessions[] AND normalized sessions[] is empty → preserve absent form + top-level orchestrator/startedAt passthrough); both cancelSessionSet and restoreSessionSet wrap their final writeSessionState call through toV4OnDiskShape. tsc --noEmit clean.

Added SessionSetPrerequisite type to types.ts ({slug: string, condition: 'complete'}). Extended SessionSet record with prerequisites (parsed from spec.md) + blockedByPrereqs (derived). parsePrerequisites added to fileSystem.ts as a lightweight regex parser (no YAML lib dependency): returns null when field absent, [] when explicit prerequisites:[], or the parsed list otherwise; tolerant of operator typos. readSessionSets cross-references each set's prereqs against the target set's state via a post-loop pass that builds setsByName map first; ANY unsatisfied prereq blocks the row, unknown target slug also blocks (typo / missing-set must not silently unblock). Updated 5 test-fixture files (actionRegistry, flagDecisionForReview, forceClosedBadge, inProgressSetsService, sessionSetsProvider) to include the new fields with null/false defaults.

Added blockedByPrereqsBadge predicate to SessionSetsModel.ts: returns '[BLOCKED BY PREREQS]' on non-terminal rows where set.blockedByPrereqs=true; suppressed on complete/cancelled rows (once a set is closed, its dependency status is no longer actionable). Wired into CustomSessionSetsView.descriptionFor's extras .filter(Boolean) chain alongside touchedDate/uatBadge/forceClosedBadge/needsMigrationBadge. Also extended contextValueFor to append :blocked-by-prereqs on non-terminal rows so future right-click menu actions can gate on it.

Authored sessionStateV4Writers.test.ts (12 tests across the four writer surfaces' v4 emission + plan-less carve-out + cancel/restore round-trip — covers schemaVersion=4, dropped top-level keys absent, per-session metadata defaulting, headings fallback, change-log vs activity-log branches, plan-less cancel/restore preservation). Authored prerequisites.test.ts (10 tests covering parsePrerequisites shape variants + readSessionSets cross-reference + blockedByPrereqsBadge predicate). Updated baseline tests for v4 emission: cancelLifecycle.test.ts (writer-parity test → v4 cancel shape with dropped top-level keys + per-session orchestrator preserved on session 1), fileSystem.test.ts (3 lazy-synth tests now assert schemaVersion=4 / no top-level lifecycleState / per-session startedAt for activity-log branch). Discovered + fixed parsePrerequisites entry-regex bug: lookahead /\s*$/ truncated multi-line entries at first whitespace-only EOL; switched to split-based extraction.

Authored blocked-by-prereqs.spec.ts as 3 Layer-3 Playwright scenarios (later expanded to 4 in the verifier-fix step): (1) dependant renders [BLOCKED BY PREREQS] when prereq target is in-progress, (2) badge clears when prereq target flipped to complete, (3) badge absent when prerequisites field is not declared. Each scenario builds a two-set fixture via the harness's makeSet + appendPrerequisitesToSpec helper (inline regex replacement to inject prereqs into the existing yaml block), then launches Electron VS Code and asserts the rendered row description text. setStatusToComplete helper for scenario 2 forges a v4-shape complete state on disk.
